content: We can frame Self-Refine as the repeated 'act of stepping back to critique' and Reflexion as the structured long-term memory that stores critiques across multiple separate tasks, forming a hybrid autonomous improvement loop where Self-Refine handles in-task output polishing and Reflexion accumulates generalized self-critique rules to speed up refinement for future tasks. This hybrid framework retains the key advantage of both approaches: no extra supervised training or external reward signals are required at any stage.
